title: Malformed percent-encoding in Cookie headers can crash auth request handling
reasoning: decodeURIComponent throws URIError for malformed percent escapes such as '%E0%A4%A'. parseCookies does not catch that exception, and rp.ts calls parseCookies while reading session cookies on normal auth paths. A client can send a malformed Cookie header and turn otherwise safe unauthenticated/session reads into an unhandled exception instead of a null session or ignored malformed cookie.
reproduction: Call parseCookies('session=%E0%A4%A') or request /api/auth/me with Cookie: <session-name>=%E0%A4%A; decodeURIComponent throws before the auth handler can return a normal JSON response.
recommendation: Catch decodeURIComponent failures per cookie entry and skip the malformed cookie value, or preserve the raw value when decoding fails.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The parseCookies tests cover missing equals signs and empty names, but not invalid percent-encoded values that make decodeURIComponent throw.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a parseCookies test with an invalid percent escape and assert it does not throw and ignores that cookie entry.
minimumFixScope: gdg-lib/src/auth/cookie.ts parseCookies plus one cookie.test.ts regression.
